rationale: Both candidates independently catch the trust_remote_code default flip (True→False), the one issue the PR author himself flagged as a real bug. But Y is the only one that surfaces and validates the central, most-repeated reviewer concern — that endpoint restrictions must be resolved from the final pipeline after deploy-config override, not the auto-detected one — correctly noting the regression test added for it, which was the dominant thread across amy-why-3459, hsliuustc0106, and the author. X's findings are well-evidenced and highly actionable (explicit 'add try/except', 'add a test' fixes with line-level citations) but are all secondary/tangential (missing try/except on load_deploy_config, a lost warning message, cache poisoning on None), and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict with multiple 'major' tags overstates severity relative to the PR's actual approved/merged trajectory. Precision and actionability are roughly comparable between the two, tipping this to a slight win for Y on recall of the concern reviewers actually cared about most.
